title: Today's Tech Brief: AI Animated TV and Malicious Packages
summary: Amazon's AI animated TV show and malicious npm packages
imageAlt: Image from source article for Amazon Is Making an AI-Animated ‘Good Advice Cupcake’ TV Show. Its Original Creator Is Furious
imageCaption: Amazon Is Making an AI-Animated ‘Good Advice Cupcake’ TV Show. Its Original Creator Is Furious
imageSourceTitle: Amazon Is Making an AI-Animated ‘Good Advice Cupcake’ TV Show. Its Original Creator Is Furious

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm excited to share some interesting tech news with you today. Let's dive right in. We've got a couple of stories that caught my attention this week, and I think you'll find them pretty fascinating.

Next, a lone attacker published 14 malicious npm packages that mimicked popular OpenSearch and Elasticsearch libraries. Luckily, Microsoft caught wind of these and took them down. It's a good reminder that even in the world of open-source software, security threats can pop up in unexpected places. It's like finding a bad apple in the bunch – you never know where it might be hiding.

And, on a related note, I stumbled upon another story about the increasing use of AI in animation. While it's amazing to see how far AI technology has come, it's also raising questions about ownership and control. I mean, who owns a character that's been brought to life using AI?

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
